revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Heike Nokyo]][[ja:平家納経]]
{{Short description|Collection of Buddhist religious texts}} {{for|the Heike Monogatari|The Tale of the Heike}} {{Use dmy dates|date=August 2022}} [[File:Heikenoukyou.jpg|thumb|Heikenoukyou]] The '''Heike Nōkyō''' {{lang|ja|平家納経}}, is a collection of {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|qid=Q748|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism}} {{ill|Religious text|en|Religious text|qid=Q179461|lt=Religious text|1=Religious text}}s in Japan from the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. These texts include 33 scrolls of the {{ill|en:Lotus Sutra|en|en:Lotus Sutra|qid=Q861212|lt=en:Lotus Sutra|1=Lotus Sutra}}, one [[:en:Amitabha Sutra|Amitabha Sutra]] scroll, one {{ill|Heart Sutra|en|Heart Sutra|qid=Q622868|lt=Heart Sutra|1=Heart Sutra (interlinear translation)}} scroll and one prayer scroll dedicated to the [[Itsukushima Shrine]].<ref>Rites and Rule: Kiyomori at Itsukushima and Fukuhara, Heather Blair, June 2013, Vol. 73, No. 1, p.22</ref> The Nōkyō is written in a form of Japanese known in English as {{ill|Classical Japanese|en|Classical Japanese|qid=Q1332057|lt=Classical Japanese|1=Classical Japanese}}.
It has had a profound influence on [[:en:Japanese culture|Japanese culture]] and {{ill|Japanese art|en|Japanese art|qid=Q603399|lt=Japanese art|1=Japanese art}} and was created during a [[:en:Golden age|Golden age]] of Japanese {{ill|Art history|en|Art history|qid=Q50637|lt=Art history|1=Art history}}. It is one of the masterpieces of decorated sutras ( sôshoku kyô ) of the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} {{ill|Emakimono|en|Emakimono|qid=Q1190781|lt=Emakimono|1=Emakimono}} texts and is considered a [[:en:National Treasure of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]].<ref>The term "{{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|qid=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}" has been used in Japan to denote {{ill|Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q858308|lt=cultural properties|1=Cultural Property (Japan)}} since 1897. Architecture and authority in Japan, William Howard Coaldrake, 1996[2002], p.248, ISBN 0-415-05754-X, Routledge| See [https://books.google.com/books?id=bCLNX8_a4WQC&pg=PA248] </ref>
== History and development == The Nōkyō was commissioned by the [[Taira clan]] and dedicated by Taira no Kiyomori (dd18-81CE) to Itsukushima Shrine in dd64CE in a prayer scroll (Heike Nogyo).<ref>The Buddhist Mythmaking of Defilement: Sacred Courtesans in Medieval Japan, Michele Marra, The Journal of Asian Studies, February 1993, Vol. 52, No. 1, p.55</ref> It was made by aristocratic circles in the 12th century and depicts the tale of the [[Taira clan|Heike]], numbering 33 to represent the 33 goddesses found at Itsukushima who were said to take the form of [[Kannon]].<ref>Rites and Rule: Kiyomori at Itsukushima and Fukuhara, Heather Blair, June 2013, Vol. 73, No. 1, p.22</ref><ref>Disguised Scripts and Hidden Poems in an Illustrated Heian Sutra: Ashide and Uta-E in the Heike Nōgyō, Julia Meech-Pekarik, Archives of Asian Art , 1977/1978, Vol. 31, p. 53</ref> The Nokyo was written in Classical Japanese, {{ill|Classical Chinese|en|Classical Chinese|qid=Q37041|lt=Classical Chinese|1=Classical Chinese}} and the {{ill|Kana|en|Kana|qid=Q187659|lt=Kana|1=Kana}} script formatted as Ashida-E (reed writing) representing a votive and religious work of Heian-specific Art.<ref>Disguised Scripts and Hidden Poems in an Illustrated Heian Sutra: Ashide and Uta-E in the Heike Nōgyō, Julia Meech-Pekarik, Archives of Asian Art , 1977/1978, Vol. 31, p. 53</ref>
In 1602, the [[:en:Rinpa|Rinpa]] painter [[:en:Tawaraya Sotatsu|Tawaraya Sotatsu]] was commissioned to restore the Nokyo which affected his painting.<ref>Encyclopedia of world biography. Vol. 17, Paula K Byers, 1998, Index, ISBN 0787622214, London, Gale</ref>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:HEIKE Lotus Sutra Prologue.JPG|Lotus Sutra Prologue File:Heike-Nokyo-Chapter-12-Lotus-Sutra.png|Chapter 12 Lotus Sutra File:Heikenoukyou.jpg|Woman from Chapter 12 </gallery>
== See also == * [[:en:Tale of Genji|Tale of Genji]] * {{ill|Japanese painting|en|Japanese painting|qid=Q1683295|lt=Japanese painting|1=Japanese painting}} * {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} * [[:en:History of Japanese art|History of Japanese art]] * [[Nara Research Institute for Cultural Properties]] * [[:en:Tokyo Research Institute for Cultural Properties|Tokyo Research Institute for Cultural Properties]]
